methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Ambient Photonics is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Bates Marshall and Elin Topp that has raised $68M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-sector, same-stage private companies including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., alongside one public comparable (Lanzatech, ticker LNZA). The peer universe ranges from $120M to $5.0B in valuation, with a median of $355M.

Two triangulation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $68M capital raised proxy, implying a $129M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the six identified comparables yields $355M, reflecting the heterogeneous risk and commercialization profiles within the climate-tech sector. The wide peer range ($120M–$5.0B) reflects material dispersion in technology maturity and capital intensity across the cohort.

Triangulated results support a valuation range of $110M to $408M, with a median of $242M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median methods—driven by Ambient Photonics' earlier-stage capital raise relative to median peer valuations—represents the primary uncertainty. Resolution depends on commercialization progress, unit economics, and market validation relative to more mature peer cohort members.
